objective: Build dense point-level semantic assets from scanner data by keeping geometry ownership clean first, then accumulating visual and VLM evidence as semantic posterior signals.

current_diagnosis.summary: The recent v15/v16 branches made large parts of ground and wall unknown because geometry guards were applied as hard semantic demotion rules on top of an already weak v12 semantic/coarsening base.
current_diagnosis.decision: Do not continue tuning v12/v14/v15/v16 as the main semantic baseline. Treat them as diagnostics proving that semantic repair rules cannot compensate for impure patch/object ownership.

active_baselines.description: Validated first-touch/full-scene visibility route with pure surface visibility; useful as semantic and projection sanity reference.
Earlier structure-prior plus VLM/MASK semantic teacher. It is not a geometry ownership solution, but remains the strongest local semantic teacher.
Teacher transfer from v20 onto geometry patch objects without changing point ownership. Useful diagnostic; not yet final due patch fragmentation and partial teacher coverage.

rejected_artifacts.reason: Grid6 coarsening merged more fragments, but semantic teacher errors were amplified; visual QA showed ground labeled as wall and car hood labeled as floor.
Geometry guard allowed wall-to-floor promotion for horizontal/up-normal conflicts and made many wall-like regions appear as floor.
Disabled wall-to-floor promotion, but over-demoted large surface content to unknown.
Reduced small-surface demotion, but still left high point-level unknown and did not solve weak v12 ownership/semantic base.
Global object back-projection VLM relabel produced wrong evidence and points on sky/far buildings; not a mainline semantic source.
Uses an unverified coordinate/projection chain; superseded by img_pos + cam_in_ex + undistort + first-touch visibility.
Video frames and lidar keyframes are not strict one-to-one pairs; production route must project full world cloud through camera poses.
Direct SAM semantic PNG voting on tiny patch objects gave poor labels and should only be evidence, not ownership or final classification.

architecture_principles: Geometry ownership first: each voxel belongs to exactly one patch/object before semantic labeling.
Semantic evidence cannot define object boundaries by itself.
VLM, SAM, DINO, scene prior, and structural region fields are evidence sources, not truth sources.
drivability_cpp output is a structural compatibility prior only; it cannot prove floor, wall, or ceiling semantics.
Use dense raw LAS or dense voxel stores for production logic; stride10 viewer exports are QA artifacts only.
Rejected diagnostic artifacts must not become implicit defaults in scripts or viewer links.
If a rule repeatedly fixes one class while breaking another, stop adding patch rules and return to the ownership/evidence model.

next_mainline.step: Build dense 0.03m feature store from raw Opt LAS
next_mainline.purpose: Create production geometry/appearance source with xyz, color, normals, roughness, local spectral features, and frame/visibility metadata.

next_mainline.step: Generate clean geometry patches with exclusive voxel ownership
next_mainline.purpose: Patch formation should use spatial reachability plus local model compatibility, not semantic labels.

next_mainline.step: Accumulate first-touch visual evidence onto patches
next_mainline.purpose: Collect skymask, RGB, depth consistency, 2D mask overlap, and VLM descriptions as patch evidence.
Assign canonical labels only when geometry, structure prior, visual evidence, and VLM description converge; otherwise keep ambiguous/unknown.
